title: deferred playbook executor with scheduled dry-run and safe rollback hooks
preview: Motivation: On-call automation needs a way to schedule runbooks to execute outside peak windows, run a non-destructive dry-run before live execution, and ensure safe automatic rollback when automation makes state changes. This PR adds a Deferred Playbook Executor that accepts scheduled windows and optional dry-run p...
text: Motivation: On-call automation needs a way to schedule runbooks to execute outside peak windows, run a non-destructive dry-run before live execution, and ensure safe automatic rollback when automation makes state changes. This PR adds a Deferred Playbook Executor that accepts scheduled windows and optional dry-run payloads, a scheduled dry-run runner that simulates playbook steps without side effects, and pluggable safe-rollback hooks that capture pre-change snapshots and provide an automated revert path. Key design points: (1) All deferred executions are stored as immutable scheduling tasks with idempotency tokens, (2) dry-run mode routes actions to a simulator layer that returns predicted outcomes and a list of mutated resources, (3) rollback hooks register resource snapshotters and revert actions, and (4) RBAC/feature-flag gating prevents accidental live scheduling. This change als...

title: Introduce randomized counterfactual probe and soft-fail CI adapter
preview: Adds a randomized counterfactual probe to the eval-harness and a soft-fail CI adapter that surfaces regressions without hard-blocking merges unless they exceed configurable statistical thresholds. Motivation: many regressions are brittle to minor prompt edits; counterfactual probing produces small, semantically pres...
text: Adds a randomized counterfactual probe to the eval-harness and a soft-fail CI adapter that surfaces regressions without hard-blocking merges unless they exceed configurable statistical thresholds. Motivation: many regressions are brittle to minor prompt edits; counterfactual probing produces small, semantically preserving edits and measures sensitivity. Design: probe generator implements configurable perturbation strategies (entity swap, tense shift, negation flip, punctuation perturbation) with deterministic seeding for reproducibility. The CI adapter runs a small batched probe set against the PR head, computes a bootstrap-based p-value and effect-size estimate, and returns either OK, WARN, or FAIL based on thresholds. WARN is marked as a soft-failure that appears prominently in PR checks and in the console, encouraging fixes without stopping fast-moving teams. Includes CLI flags --c...

title: Introduce timebox seeder and hierarchical telemetry compactifier for long-run benchmarks
preview: Motivation: Long-duration benchmark runs in Benchmark Lab are noisy and expensive to store because we capture full-event traces and per-sample payloads for every iteration. Two common pain points: (1) realistic warm-cache steady-state is expensive to reach deterministically across different sequence-length mixes; (2...
text:
Motivation: Long-duration benchmark runs in Benchmark Lab are noisy and expensive to store because we capture full-event traces and per-sample payloads for every iteration. Two common pain points: (1) realistic warm-cache steady-state is expensive to reach deterministically across different sequence-length mixes; (2) raw telemetry volume for long runs (multi-hour stress / soak) is large, which slows downstream analysis and inflates storage costs.

Design & changes: Introduce a timebox seeder (timebox-seeder) that drives a configurable, bounded priming stage prior to each measurement epoch. The seeder supports: target distributions (seq-length, token-priming prefixes, model-mix), controlled eviction pressure knobs, and deterministic seeding modes (seed schedules can be reused across runs for reproducibility). Parallel to the seeder, add a hierarchical telemetry compactifier (rollup-tel...

title: Add benchmark serializer and release timeliner
preview: Why: onboarding produces many per-variant benchmark artifacts (latency histograms, P50/P95 traces, throughput stamps). We need a compact, versioned on-disk format so the registry can store, index, and compose those artifacts into human-facing release notes and machine-checkable regression timelines. This PR introduc...
text:
Why: onboarding produces many per-variant benchmark artifacts (latency histograms, P50/P95 traces, throughput stamps). We need a compact, versioned on-disk format so the registry can store, index, and compose those artifacts into human-facing release notes and machine-checkable regression timelines. This PR introduces a small, portable 'benchmark serializer' and a 'release timeliner' service that composes micro-release entries into a chronological digest for changelogs and downgrade/rollout playbooks.

What: single PR that implements: (1) bench serializer (binary+JSON compact representation with v1 header and stable field ordering); (2) timeliner CLI and library that consumes serialized benchmark artifacts and emits a microrelease timeline (JSONL) and a condensed markdown digest for release notes; (3) CI hook to assemble timeline artifacts and attach a release digest draft to the line...

title: Add bounded-confidence replay CI hook and progressive quarantine
preview: Motivation: intermittent prompt-set regressions commonly triggered by sampling noise result in noisy CI failures and unnecessary quarantines. This change introduces a bounded-confidence replay CI hook that adaptively replays failing cohorts until a statistically sound confidence interval for the failure rate is obta...
text: Motivation: intermittent prompt-set regressions commonly triggered by sampling noise result in noisy CI failures and unnecessary quarantines. This change introduces a bounded-confidence replay CI hook that adaptively replays failing cohorts until a statistically sound confidence interval for the failure rate is obtained (Wilson score interval) or until a configured cap is reached. The hook also integrates a progressive-quarantine policy that transitions prompt cohorts through soft->hard quarantine states based on posterior confidence rather than raw failure counts. Design: ci/hooks/bounded_confidence_replay.py implements the replay loop, statistics and retry backoff; ci/policies/progressive_quarantine.yaml contains policy knobs (min_samples, max_replays, target_ci_width, soft_threshold, hard_threshold, decay). We expose a CLI toggle (--ci-bounded-replay) and a feature flag in CI confi...

title: add-frequency-domain-residuals-detector-and-graceful-threshold-adapter
preview: Motivation: transient, periodic interference (GC sweeps, noisy neighbors, periodic telemetry jobs) has caused low-amplitude latency regressions that percentile-only detectors occasionally miss and that can trigger noisy rollbacks. This PR introduces a new detector that analyzes latency time-series in the frequency d...
text: Motivation: transient, periodic interference (GC sweeps, noisy neighbors, periodic telemetry jobs) has caused low-amplitude latency regressions that percentile-only detectors occasionally miss and that can trigger noisy rollbacks. This PR introduces a new detector that analyzes latency time-series in the frequency domain and fuses spectral residual signals with existing percentile drift metrics to produce a more stable canary signal. Design summary: 1) Windowed FFT transform of per-request p95/p99 time series over short sliding windows (configurable); 2) Compute spectral residual energy in targeted bands (e.g., 0.5-5s cycles) and normalize by traffic power to be traffic-aware; 3) Produce a residuals_score time series and combine with percentile delta via a probabilistic fusion step (logistic weight based on signal SNR); 4) Graceful Threshold Adapter: thresholds are scaled by a lightwe...

title: catalog: surface model capability flags, pricing and limits for OpenAI-compat clients
preview: Motivation: Clients using the OpenAI-compat layer and our public model listing need a single, predictable surface to discover per-model capabilities (streaming stability, function/tool-calling support, structured-output specializations) and unit economics (pricing per token, soft/hard limits and concurrency). This P...
text:
Motivation: Clients using the OpenAI-compat layer and our public model listing need a single, predictable surface to discover per-model capabilities (streaming stability, function/tool-calling support, structured-output specializations) and unit economics (pricing per token, soft/hard limits and concurrency). This PR extends the model catalog API and internal catalog objects to include capability and pricing metadata, plus server-side filters that power the console and SDKs.

Summary of changes: added explicit capability flags (openai_compat, supports_streaming_stable, supports_function_calling, structured_output_profiles) to the model listing; exposed priced_units and limits metadata (cost_per_input_token, cost_per_output_token, max_tokens_per_request, recommended_concurrency, rate_limit_tier). Implemented new query params for the GET /v1/models endpoint to filter by capability, max_...

title: Introduce probabilistic span sampler and kernel branch-counter hooks for adaptive scheduler insights
preview: Context and motivation: We see intermittent scheduler degradation for long-tail requests where KV cache hits are correlated with kernel branch behavior and micro-stalls. Existing NVTX and epoch counters capture timing but not branch-level dynamics. This PR introduces a probabilistic (weighted) span sampler that bias...
text:
Context and motivation: We see intermittent scheduler degradation for long-tail requests where KV cache hits are correlated with kernel branch behavior and micro-stalls. Existing NVTX and epoch counters capture timing but not branch-level dynamics. This PR introduces a probabilistic (weighted) span sampler that biases capture toward requests with high KV lookup latency and late-batch positions, plus a small set of kernel branch/mispredict counters exposed through the telemetry pipeline so dashboard panels can correlate scheduler decisions with low-level CPU/GPU branch behavior.

What this PR does (single-string description): Adds a lightweight probabilistic span sampler (runtime/sampling/weighted_span_sampler.{cc,h}) that samples at enqueue time based on configurable weights: kv_hit_latency (ms), batch_index, and recent_retry_count. The sampler emits full NVTX-range + span payload for...

title: Hybrid quant execution paths: FP8-aware kernels, INT8 dequant+matmul fusion and scheduler refactor
preview: Motivation: customers running large conversational workloads push two axes of optimization simultaneously: cheaper quantized kernels (INT8/FP8) and smarter scheduling to preserve latency SLOs when batching. This PR implements a hybrid execution strategy that (1) adds FP8-aware matmul kernels with per-channel scale m...
text: Motivation: customers running large conversational workloads push two axes of optimization simultaneously: cheaper quantized kernels (INT8/FP8) and smarter scheduling to preserve latency SLOs when batching. This PR implements a hybrid execution strategy that (1) adds FP8-aware matmul kernels with per-channel scale metadata, (2) implements an INT8 dequant+matmul fusion execution path to avoid intermediate FP buffers when upstream weights are INT8 and downstream ops permit FP accumulation, and (3) refactors the request scheduler to expose an adaptive batch window and preemptive slotting to reduce head-of-line and tail latency during bursty traffic. Summary of changes: new kernel files under serving/runtime/kernels/{fp8_matmul.cc,fp8_matmul.h,quant_fusion.cc}, changes to kernel registry to prefer fused path when data layout and accumulation type are compatible, scheduler refactor in serv...

title: Introduce soft-label probing and calibrated-expectation CI hook
preview: Context and motivation: Some regressions manifest as shifts in the model's probability mass over semantically equivalent answers rather than hard label changes. Existing binary pass/fail gates can be too brittle (false positives) or miss subtle quality drift. This PR adds a 'soft-label prober' and a calibrated-expec...
text:
Context and motivation: Some regressions manifest as shifts in the model's probability mass over semantically equivalent answers rather than hard label changes. Existing binary pass/fail gates can be too brittle (false positives) or miss subtle quality drift. This PR adds a 'soft-label prober' and a calibrated-expectation CI hook that (a) probes prompt cohorts with synthetic soft labels (probability distributions over acceptable outputs), (b) compares aggregate likelihood mass assigned to expected semantic clusters, and (c) uses a noise-aware historical model to set dynamic tolerances so teams get fewer spurious CI failures while retaining sensitivity to true regressions.

Goals: 1) detect semantic drift that doesn't appear as single-answer mismatches; 2) reduce noisy CI failures by modeling historical variance per-cohort; 3) provide clear remediation artifacts (example worst-case pro...

title: orchestrate-credential-fabric-and-async-generator-bridge
preview: Context: Multiple SDK teams requested a small, consistent surface for credential fallback and streaming ergonomics. Customers reported integration friction when migrating long-lived streaming consumers (chat/observable pipelines) between Python/TS/Go — token refresh timing, retry floor semantics, and typed async gen...
text: Context: Multiple SDK teams requested a small, consistent surface for credential fallback and streaming ergonomics. Customers reported integration friction when migrating long-lived streaming consumers (chat/observable pipelines) between Python/TS/Go — token refresh timing, retry floor semantics, and typed async generator shapes diverged. This PR introduces the "credential fabric" concept: a lightweight orchestration layer that centralizes credential selection/fallback, prefetch 'stitch' mode, and a small compatibility shim that yields a unified async-generator/iterator surface across languages. Motivation: reduce app-side glue, enable shared retry presets, and make typed stream consumers portable across SDKs without breaking existing integrations. Summary of changes: - Introduces a credential-fabric layer in the monorepo that exports a language-agnostic contract for: preferred creden...

title: streaming-reconstruction-guard-and-toolcall-stable-handback
preview: Motivation: We observed a class of in-production incidents where SSE fragment splits inside escaped JSON strings or multi-byte UTF-8 sequences could cause downstream consumers to see re-ordered function/tool call boundaries or emit malformed partial JSON. This PR introduces a lightweight runtime 'reconstruction guar...
text: Motivation: We observed a class of in-production incidents where SSE fragment splits inside escaped JSON strings or multi-byte UTF-8 sequences could cause downstream consumers to see re-ordered function/tool call boundaries or emit malformed partial JSON. This PR introduces a lightweight runtime 'reconstruction guard' that assigns per-fragment fidelity credits and a deterministic handback protocol to ensure toolcall boundaries remain ordered across retransmit/replay and partial-frame repair. Summary of changes: 1) runtime/streaming: add FragmentFidelityLedger and a reconstruction guard that tracks credit windows and stalls handback until a safe boundary is observed or the ledger times out; 2) sse/parser: introduce escape-aware tail rewriter to avoid splitting inside escapes and normalize trailing delimiters before emission; 3) compat/openai: emit X-Redwood-Handback-Fidelity header and...

title: Add cohort-aware spiral prober and rollback curator for transient regression isolation
preview: Motivation: We repeatedly see short-lived latency spikes that trigger standard canaries and cause unnecessary rollbacks. This PR implements a cohort-aware probing strategy ("spiral prober") plus a lightweight rollback curator that isolates transient regressions from persistent degradations and provides a staged roll...
text: Motivation: We repeatedly see short-lived latency spikes that trigger standard canaries and cause unnecessary rollbacks. This PR implements a cohort-aware probing strategy ("spiral prober") plus a lightweight rollback curator that isolates transient regressions from persistent degradations and provides a staged rollback budget for safe cutovers. The change is intended to reduce noise-driven rollbacks while preserving sensitivity to real regressions.

title: hackathon: add internal LLM routing prototype
preview: Motivation: for the internal hackathon we built a small routing microservice that demonstrates routing inference requests across two lightweight model variants (fast/cheap vs accurate/slow) based on simple latency and cost heuristics. This is a demo/prototype only and intended to seed a conversation about smarter ro...
text:
Motivation: for the internal hackathon we built a small routing microservice that demonstrates routing inference requests across two lightweight model variants (fast/cheap vs accurate/slow) based on simple latency and cost heuristics. This is a demo/prototype only and intended to seed a conversation about smarter routing policies that could later be integrated into the runtime.

What this PR does: adds a toy HTTP microservice (Python/Flask) that accepts Redwood-style request payloads, applies a configurable policy to choose a target model variant, forwards the request to a mock backend adapter, and returns annotated response metadata. Includes a README with run instructions, a small policy DSL (yaml) and unit tests for policy evaluation.

Key design notes: the service is intentionally minimal: policy evaluation is synchronous and in-process, there is no persistence, and backends are m...

title: Introduce adaptive pager suppression using noise profiling
preview: Motivation: paging fatigue from repetitive low-value alerts continues to erode oncall bandwidth. This change adds an adaptive pager-suppression subsystem to incident-bot that builds and uses per-service noise profiles to decide when to suppress, group, or throttle notifications. Key goals: reduce noisy low-action pa...
text:
Motivation: paging fatigue from repetitive low-value alerts continues to erode oncall bandwidth. This change adds an adaptive pager-suppression subsystem to incident-bot that builds and uses per-service noise profiles to decide when to suppress, group, or throttle notifications. Key goals: reduce noisy low-action pages by ~40% in a test cohort, provide clear feedback to oncall (why a page was suppressed), and make suppression policies configurable and auditable.

What this PR does: 
- Adds a lightweight noise profiler that accumulates sketch summaries of alert fingerprints (hash, signature dimensions) and computes short-window noise scores (noise_rate, dedupe_entropy, throttle_suggestion).
- Implements an adaptive suppression policy engine that can: suppress (no page), group (single consolidated page for an incident burst), or debounce (delay page by Xs) based on policy rules and the ...

title: Safe Rejoin Coordinator with Connection Epoch Fence
preview: Motivation: During a cross-region incident we observed a small class of degradations where connections that rejoined quickly after transient network flaps caused KV cache overwrite and high tail latency spikes. This PR introduces a Safe Rejoin Coordinator and a Connection Epoch Fence to ensure rejoining nodes valida...
text:
Motivation: During a cross-region incident we observed a small class of degradations where connections that rejoined quickly after transient network flaps caused KV cache overwrite and high tail latency spikes. This PR introduces a Safe Rejoin Coordinator and a Connection Epoch Fence to ensure rejoining nodes validate epoch affinity before accepting requests that may mutate or repopulate per-connection KV cache entries. The change reduces risk of stale-kv stomping and provides a short quiesce window for region rejoin sequences.

Scope: single runtime-side coordinator service + lightweight epoch fence protocol and control-plane signals for coordinated rejoin. The implementation intentionally keeps the runtime fast-path unchanged for steady state; the coordinator only participates during node state transitions.

What this PR changes: 1) runtime/rejoin/coordinator: new coordinator that t...

title: Introduce hermetic Go CI runtime and slimline pipeline shim
preview: Motivation: our existing CI relies on many small shell scripts invoked from workflow YAMLs; those scripts are a common source of subtle failures (environment differences, word-splitting bugs, silent pipefail issues) and make local dev/CI parity hard. This change introduces a small hermetic Go-based CI runtime (ci-ru...
text: Motivation: our existing CI relies on many small shell scripts invoked from workflow YAMLs; those scripts are a common source of subtle failures (environment differences, word-splitting bugs, silent pipefail issues) and make local dev/CI parity hard. This change introduces a small hermetic Go-based CI runtime (ci-runner) that replaces the most fragile shell entrypoints and consolidates common plumbing (workspace setup, action inputs normalization, token-safe logging, artifact staging). Goals: (1) reduce flakes by making CI entrypoints deterministic, (2) shrink workflow YAMLs to declarative stages and let the runner handle retries/backoff, (3) centralize action pinning and dependency-check hooks, and (4) pave way for incremental migration of legacy jobs. Work in this PR: - Add tools/ci/runner/main.go (single binary ~7 file packages) that implements: workspace bootstrap, env validation,...

title: model-family kernel picker + GQA/MQA tuning + Qwen KV-cache stride fix
preview: Context: we observed higher tail latency and sporadic OOMs when serving mixed workloads containing Mistral, Qwen and Llama family models. Root causes were: (1) suboptimal kernel selection for fused rotary/attention on Mistral and some Llama variants, (2) misaligned group-quantization (GQA) code paths that spilled to...
text: Context: we observed higher tail latency and sporadic OOMs when serving mixed workloads containing Mistral, Qwen and Llama family models. Root causes were: (1) suboptimal kernel selection for fused rotary/attention on Mistral and some Llama variants, (2) misaligned group-quantization (GQA) code paths that spilled to host under certain MQA layouts, and (3) a KV-cache pointer/stride bug in Qwen cache layout that created fragmentation under continuous batching. This PR introduces a model-family aware kernel picker (heuristics based on model_family, hidden_size, seq_dist) that prefers fused-rotary + threaded attention kernels for Mistral/Llama; adds quantization-aware attention execution paths to handle GQA/MQA edge cases (avoids fp16->host spills by switching to a compact packed layout when group sizes mismatch); fixes Qwen KV-cache stride/pointer arithmetic and compacts cache layout to ...

title: introduce-cost-sentinel-prefetch-catalog-and-quant-tier-optimizer
preview: Context: Customers with mixed bursty and steady traffic were seeing suboptimal unit economics from 'eager' prefetch defaults and a single global quantization policy. This change introduces a cost-aware prefetch coalescer (Cost Sentinel) that builds a lightweight tenant prefetch catalog and a Quant Tier Optimizer tha...
text: Context: Customers with mixed bursty and steady traffic were seeing suboptimal unit economics from 'eager' prefetch defaults and a single global quantization policy. This change introduces a cost-aware prefetch coalescer (Cost Sentinel) that builds a lightweight tenant prefetch catalog and a Quant Tier Optimizer that selects conservative quant profiles at rollout time. Motivation: reduce per-token cost on mid-size tenants while preserving latency SLOs and preventing quality regressions during quant rollouts. Key changes: 1) server/prefetch/cost_sentinel.py — new coalescer that synthesizes tenant prefetch catalogs from short-window request traces, implements an eviction heuristic (LRU by last-use + size score) and exposes a bounded catalog API; 2) service/econ/quant_selector.go — runtime policy to map tenant catalogs + workload shape to a quant tier (fp16 / int8-safe / int8-aggressive)...

title: Introduce quiet-merge signal and lint telemetry adapter
preview: Context: our presubmit lanes produce noisy merge traffic and noisy alerting for non-actionable lint regressions. This PR introduces two complementary pieces: 1) a lightweight 'quiet-merge' signal that lets the merge service suppress non-critical notifications and avoid triggering heavyweight post-merge jobs on low-r...
text: Context: our presubmit lanes produce noisy merge traffic and noisy alerting for non-actionable lint regressions. This PR introduces two complementary pieces: 1) a lightweight 'quiet-merge' signal that lets the merge service suppress non-critical notifications and avoid triggering heavyweight post-merge jobs on low-risk infra/docs-only changes; and 2) a lint telemetry adapter that collects anonymized lint rule violation counts and time-to-fix metrics for presubmit failures so we can prioritize rule maintenance and reduce flakiness. Motivation: reduce pager noise and CI cost from churny lint failures, enable data-driven lint rule triage, and create a simple opt-in signal for release automation to skip non-essential post-merge jobs. Work included: - new CI input flag and API hook consumed by merge-orchestrator - presubmit hook that annotates PR metadata with quiet-merge eligibility reaso...
